final_response: Filed it at `resources/prompt-cache-attacks-tldr.md` as a TLDR stub flagged "not verified against the PDF," and added a followup to pull the actual paper before next week so we don't end up citing the 73% number on the assistant's word alone.
actions: Read AGENTS.md, methodology.md, and the subfolder AGENTS.md files (daybook, projects, resources, tmp) to confirm routing conventions.

Created resources/prompt-cache-attacks-tldr.md containing the TLDR verbatim, an explicit unverified-source caveat, and open items (pull PDF, capture title/authors/venue, consider project promotion later).

notes: Routing rationale: TLDR is durable reference material the user explicitly wants to keep and use next week, so resources/ fits better than tmp/ (gitignored, untrusted) and better than projects/ (no stated goal/scope, single artifact).
Did not promote to a project: single file, no multi-session goal yet. resources/AGENTS.md explicitly allows flat single-file artifacts.
Did not write to decisions.md or observations.md: this is a routing action, not a commitment or cross-cutting pattern.
Flagged the 73% recovery-rate figure as unverified in both the resource file and the followup so it doesn't get quoted from an assistant-generated TLDR.
Handoff section in daybook left as a placeholder per daybook/AGENTS.md — to be filled at end-of-session, not mid-task.
